title: duchesnia crispa
intro: Duchesnia crispa, also known as yellow viper's bugloss, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Duchesnia crispa include relief from menstrual cramps, arthritis, and other conditions associated with inflammation and spasms. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, teas, or tinctures, and its root is sometimes dried and used as a powder or capsule.
